source.title: Towards an executive without a homunculus: computational models of the prefrontal cortex/basal ganglia system
source.venue: Philosophical Transactions of the Royal Society B: Biological Sciences

confidence_rationale: This paper is the clearest statement in the PBWM tradition of parallel BG loops across motor, cognitive, and limbic domains with shared canonical circuit logic — directly motivating anatomically separable sensorium, thought-loop, and action-commitment gates. Affective weighting via amygdala/ventral striatum supports REE hippocampal affective residue mechanism. Strong support for Q-019.

mapping.source_claim_statement: The BG's executive function over PFC is an evolutionary extension of its motor control role: the same striatum-SNr-thalamus-frontal disinhibition circuit runs in anatomically parallel loops across motor, cognitive, and limbic/motivational domains, with dopamine training Go/NoGo gating policy for each loop independently.
mapping.ree_translation: Motor BG loop = action commitment gate (motor execution authority loop); PFC cognitive loop = thought loop gate (E3 complex WM and planning operations); sensory/attentional input filtering loop = sensorium gate (what enters the pipeline for E3 attention). Affective/motivational Pavlovian signal from amygdala = hippocampal affective residue shaping sensorium gate weighting.
mapping.mapping_caveat: Paper uses motor vs cognitive vs limbic as loop taxonomy; REE uses sensorium vs thought vs commitment. The mapping is functional and not one-to-one anatomically. Transfer risk: review paper with computational modeling basis.
mapping.source_context: Phil Trans R Soc B, 2007. Review and extension of PBWM, situating BG loops in evolutionary framework.

confidence_components.notes: High source quality (key review in the field). Highest mapping fidelity of the six papers for the three-gate loop taxonomy. Moderate transfer risk from computational model to biological implementation detail.
